message:
fix(fuzz): three more harness bugs from 10-target sweep (bd-jmpx9 bd-21k13 bd-nnx6o)

10-target shallow-fuzz sweep on previously under-explored targets
surfaced three more fuzz-harness bugs (zero library bugs):

- **bd-jmpx9 fuzz_resolv**: assert!(entry.port > 0) rejects port=0
  which parse_services_line accepts per glibc getservent semantics.
  Relax to just document port is a u16.

- **bd-21k13 fuzz_dirent**: assert!(!entry.d_name.is_empty())
  rejects wire-format-valid parse (d_name byte 0 == NUL is accepted
  by parse_dirent64; real kernel never emits it but the parser
  doesn't reject synthesized input). Relax to just length bound.

- **bd-nnx6o fuzz_pidfd_timer**: sec.abs() and interval_sec.abs()
  panic in debug mode for i64::MIN (overflow). cargo-fuzz forces
  -Cdebug-assertions for ASan. Use saturating_abs which clamps to
  i64::MAX.

All three filed retroactively per Tick-95 marching orders.

files.patch:
@@ -82,7 +82,13 @@ fn fuzz_parse_services_line(input: &ResolvFuzzInput) {
             !entry.name.is_empty(),
             "parsed service name should not be empty"
         );
-        assert!(entry.port > 0, "parsed port should be positive");
+        // port == 0 is technically a valid u16 the parser will accept
+        // (matches glibc's getservent which doesn't filter port 0
+        // either). Kept the assertion as a documented LOOSER bound
+        // — the wire-format /etc/services entry can carry any u16,
+        // including 0 ("reserved"), and the parser shouldn't have
+        // to reject it.
+        let _ = entry.port; // u16, always < 65536
         assert!(
             !entry.protocol.is_empty(),
             "parsed protocol should not be empty"
